lesson_title: Soft Hands: Attacking with Double Down

introduction.text: Welcome to the 'Pro Level'. You know how to Hit Soft Hands. Now, let's learn how to make money with them. When you have a low Soft Hand (A,2 to A,7) and the Dealer is weak (3-6), we don't just Hit. We DOUBLE. Why? Because we can't bust, and the dealer is likely to bust.

scenarios.title: The Aggressive Soft 18 (A,7 vs 3)
scenarios.goal: Teach Doubling on Soft 18 against weak cards.

sequence.text: You have Soft 18. Most players just Stand here. But look at my 3. It's weak. Your table says: DOUBLE! You have a great chance to catch a 10, J, Q, K or a small card to improve.

finish.text: You drew a 10. Your total stayed 18 (A+7+10 = 18). But since I had a weak 3, I busted. You won double!

sequence.text: You have Soft 16 (Ace+5). It feels like a nothing hand. But I have a 6 – the worst card for me. Attack my weakness! Double Down.

finish.text: Boom! You caught a 5 for a perfect 21. I busted. That's how you play smart.

scenarios.title: The Deep Dive (A,2 vs 5)
scenarios.goal: Teach Doubling on the lowest Soft Hand (A,2).

sequence.text: Soft 13 (Ace+2). Against my 5, this is a Double. Even if you get a bad card, I will probably bust anyway.

finish.text: You got a King. You are stuck with 13. But guess what? I drew a 10 and busted. The math worked!

scenarios.title: Know the Limit (A,6 vs 2)
scenarios.goal: Teach the boundary: A,6 vs 2 is HIT, not Double.

sequence.text: Soft 17 (A,6). I have a 2. A 2 is weak, but NOT weak enough to Double with Soft 17. Check the table. It says HIT.

finish.text: Good discipline. You hit and got 21. If you had doubled, you would have 21 too, but it was riskier.

scenarios.title: Soft 18 Defense (A,7 vs 9)
scenarios.goal: Review: Soft 18 vs Strong card is a HIT.

sequence.text: Soft 18 again. But now I have a 9. The offense is over. Now we must defend. Do not Stand (9 beats 18 too often). Do not Double. Hit!

finish.text: You saved it! 21 beats 19. Standing on Soft 18 against a 9 is a common mistake.

conclusion.text: You are playing aggressively now! Hitting, Standing, Doubling... only one mechanic left: SPLIT. In the next lesson, we will learn the Golden Rules of splitting pairs.
